creator: trevor@jpj.net
text:
> I'd like to note that removal of Attics was only done once in the last
> two years, that I know of, in the ports tree.  So there is no "practice"
> that you speak of, just one particular overzealous removal that needs to
> be fixed at some point or another [1].

I have been told that it was done in the past, periodically.  Regardless,
I wish it would not be done any more.

> The simplest method for doing this, as I learned from FUJISHIMA-san,
> is to simply recreate blank files for each file in their old location,
> and do a cvs rm + cvs commit.  This will DTRT for cvsup and cvs too.
> Plus it will only cost us in inodes on the cvsup servers and cvs
> repositories.  Space will only be lost in a very very small amount.

I was told that conserving disk space was not the reason for the removal
of the attics.  Rather, the justification was that it would save time.
Personally, I would rather have the actual attic files restored.  That
would make it possible (modulo chaos from files which were purged but
since have undergone a "cvs add") for someone to check out a copy of the
ports tree as it was in the past, or to read the history.  Being able to
do these things is the whole point of using CVS.  The files in
http://people.freebsd.org/~trevor/ports/attics-all.tar.bz2 closely
correspond to the ones which were removed.  Adding them all back would
take on the order of 160 to 240 MB, to my (vague) recollection.

[...]

> My only problem at this point is that I do not think this patch will
> help people with cvsup.  CVSup's design, if I understand it correctly,
> is such that the only way to solve the problem is by recreating the
> attic files.  Because at the moment, cvsup will *not* delete any files
> it doesn't find in the repository.  They MUST be in the Attic.

From reading Dan's account, I suspect that the user he was chatting with
ended up having pkg/DESCR, pkg/COMMENT, and other files characteristic of
the old layout, even after cvsupping (on freefall, the patches/ and pkg/
directories do not exist for vim5).  I agree that my patch will not help
in this situation.  However, the situation for which it was written,
namely people who run cvs without the -P option so they get empty pkg/ and
patches/ directories, still exists. If the attic files are restored, it
will continue to exist indefinitely (or until cvs users have been punished
so many times that we all use -P).  If they are not restored, this patch
does no harm.

A test which uses the new layout when the pkg-descr and pkg-comment files
(required for the new layout) are present, ignoring anything under pkg/ or
patches/ and any other files from the old layout, would make it possible
to use ports even when they've suffered attic damage related to the new
layout (the purge caused other damage which can't be easily concealed).
I feel that it would just be an attempt to hide the brokenness, and to the
extent that it was successful, it would encourage future purges.  I'm not
going to offer such a bandage.
-- 
Trevor Johnson
http://jpj.net/~trevor/gpgkey.txt
